per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA states a specific method for fulfilling data portability requests, while the REG requires providing a copy of personal data undergoing processing; the texts do not clearly express the same constraint, so no quote-supported mismatch can be established.
The REA concerns how requests are fulfilled, while the REG concerns charging a reasonable fee for further copies; these are different constraints and do not directly conflict.

The REA requires fulfillment 'exclusively by printing your data on physical paper and mailing it,' while the REG says information 'may be provided orally' when requested and identity is proven; without stronger textual linkage that these govern the same obligation, the mismatch is ambiguous.
